title: Conveyor Tips Offset
description:
Conveyor Tips Offset

Offset

Pickup of moving parts

When the "pickup accuracy-priority mode" is selected for the tracking mode, the pickup position of the robot may deviate from the center of the part as shown below. This gap occurs as a result of accuracy errors of the vision system calibration, tool calibration, and tracking.

Followings are countermeasures for the problem.

Feed
	 the part with an angle near 0 degree. Then, pick up the part.

Measure
	 the gap between the center of the part and the robot's pickup position.

Repeat
	 the steps 1 and 2 for five times and calculate the average.

Set
	 the average calculated in step 3 to the program as follows:

	> Jump Cnv_QueGet(1)
	 +X(**)

Feed
	 the part with an angle near 90 degrees. Then, pick up the part.

If
	 the gap is large, fine-tune the value set in the step (4).

Feed
	 the part with an angle near 0 degree. Then, pick up the part.

If
	 the gap is large, fine-tune the value set in the step (6).

Repeat
	 the steps 6 through 8 until you get the suitable accuracy for the
	 system.

NOTE:  When the conveyor speed is 200 mm/sec, the gap between the center of the part and the pickup position of the robot cannot be smaller than ± 1 mm.

When the conveyor speed is faster than 200 mm/sec, the gap increases.

When the conveyor speed is slower than 200 mm/sec, the gap decreases.

NOTE:  If the gap between the center of the part and the pickup position can be measured by using the vision system, compensate the "Offset" as follows:

Feed
	 the part with an angle near 0 degree. Then, pick up the part.

Take
	 the image of the picked part by the camera and record X and Y coordinates.

Repeat
	 the steps 1 and 2 for five times and calculate the average.

Feed
	 the part with an angle near 90 degrees. Then, pick up the part.

Take
	 the image of the picked part by the camera and record X and Y coordinates.

Repeat
	 the steps 5 and 6 for five times and calculate the average.

Feed
	 the part with an angle near 180 degrees. Then, pick up the part.

Take
	 the image of the picked part by the camera and record X and Y coordinates.

Repeat
	 the steps 7 and 8 for five times and calculate the average.

Feed
	 the part with an angle near 270 degrees. Then, pick up the part.

Take
	 the image of the picked part by the camera and record X and Y coordinates.

Repeat
	 the steps 11 and 12 for five times and calculate the average.

Plot
	 the values in the steps 3, 6, 9, and 12 as shown in the figure below,
	 and calculate the offset value.

Set
	 the offset value to the program as follows:

	> Jump Cnv_QueGet(1)
	 +X(offset)

NOTE:  If a negative value is set to "offset", error 4406 may occur when the following program is executed.

Wait Cnv_QueLen(1, CNV_QUELEN_PICKUPAREA) > 0

Jump Cnv_QueGet(1) -X(offset)

The error can be avoided by doing either of the following:

Set the wait time before Jump command.

Set the "offset" when registering the queue and not to register when executing the Jump command.
